records.ety: [OF. estage, F. tage, (assumed) LL. staticum, from L. stare to stand. See Stand, and cf. Static.]

defs: A floor or story of a house.
An elevated platform on which an orator may speak, a play be performed, an exhibition be presented, or the like.
A floor elevated for the convenience of mechanical work, or the like; a scaffold; a staging.
A platform, often floating, serving as a kind of wharf.
The floor for scenic performances; hence, the theater; the playhouse; hence, also, the profession of representing dramatic compositions; the drama, as acted or exhibited.
quotes: Knights, squires, and steeds, must enter on the stage.
Lo! where the stage, the poor, degraded stage, Holds its warped mirror to a gaping age.

defs: A place where anything is publicly exhibited; the scene of any noted action or career; the spot where any remarkable affair occurs; as, politicians must live their lives on the public stage.
quotes: When we are born, we cry that we are come To this great stage of fools.
Music and ethereal mirth Wherewith the stage of air and earth did ring.

defs: The platform of a microscope, upon which an object is placed to be viewed. See Illust. of Microscope.
A place of rest on a regularly traveled road; a stage house; a station; a place appointed for a relay of horses.
A degree of advancement in a journey; one of several portions into which a road or course is marked off; the distance between two places of rest on a road; as, a stage of ten miles.
quotes: A stage . . . signifies a certain distance on a road.
He traveled by gig, with his wife, his favorite horse performing the journey by easy stages.

defs: A degree of advancement in any pursuit, or of progress toward an end or result.
quotes: Such a polity is suited only to a particular stage in the progress of society.

defs: A large vehicle running from station to station for the accommodation of the public; a stagecoach; an omnibus.
quotes: I went in the sixpenny stage.

defs: One of several marked phases or periods in the development and growth of many animals and plants; as, the larval stage; pupa stage; zoea stage.
To exhibit upon a stage, or as upon a stage; to display publicly.
